articles.question: How do I create an EVA account?
articles.summary: Sign up with your name, email, country, phone number, and a strong password.
articles.answer: Open eva.useaima.com and choose Sign up. Enter your full name, email, country, phone number, and a strong password, then accept the Terms and Privacy Policy. EVA creates your account and sends a verification email. After you confirm the email, EVA takes you into onboarding so your workspace starts with your real financial baseline instead of generic assumptions.

articles.question: I did not receive the verification email. What should I do?
articles.summary: Check spam first, then use resend and confirm the email address is correct.
articles.answer: First, check spam, junk, and promotions. Then return to the EVA verification screen and use Resend email. Make sure the address you entered is spelled correctly, and try to open the email in the same browser/device where you signed up. If the email still does not arrive after a few minutes, sign out, try signing up again with the correct address, and contact support@useaima.com if the problem repeats.

articles.question: Should I verify with a magic link or a code?
articles.summary: EVA supports both verification methods so you can use whichever arrives or works best for your device.
articles.answer: On the EVA verification screen, you can choose either a magic link or a verification code. A magic link is usually the fastest path because it signs you into EVA after you open the email on the same device. If your inbox shows a one-time code instead, switch EVA to verification code mode and paste it directly into the app. If a returning user signs in before confirming their email, EVA will route them back into verification so the account can be confirmed before the dashboard opens.

articles.question: What information is required during onboarding?
articles.summary: EVA uses onboarding to build your financial baseline, goals, budgets, and subscriptions.
articles.answer: Onboarding is where EVA learns your current money picture. You will confirm your financial baseline, choose your intent and guidance style, set goals, define budget limits, and list important subscriptions or financial entries when relevant. Each step is auto-saved, and you can move back to adjust earlier answers before finishing. The first spend you enter at the end becomes the first real activity in your workspace.

articles.question: Why did EVA take me back to onboarding or say it could not restore my workspace?
articles.summary: EVA now shows a workspace recovery state instead of silently throwing you back to setup when bootstrap fails.
articles.answer: If EVA cannot reload your workspace after sign-in, it should show a recovery screen with a reload option instead of assuming you are a new user. Start by using Reload workspace once. If you still land in onboarding unexpectedly, sign out and sign in again so EVA can rebuild a clean authenticated session. Auto-saved onboarding drafts stay available for the same signed-in account, so you should not need to restart from the beginning unless the account itself changed.

articles.question: How do I log a spend in EVA?
articles.summary: Tell the AI Advisor what you spent in plain language and EVA turns it into tracked spending.
articles.answer: Open AI Advisor and write your expense in plain language, for example: "I spent 20 dollars on groceries and 10 dollars on transport today." EVA parses the message, logs the spending, and shows a confirmation bubble in chat. That same approved data should then appear in Spending History, Transactions, the Dashboard, and the summaries that EVA generates from your real activity.

articles.question: My spend shows in chat but not in Spending History.
articles.summary: Refresh the workspace snapshot and confirm you are in the same signed-in account.
articles.answer: If chat says spending was logged but the rest of the workspace looks unchanged, start with a refresh of the page so EVA reloads your latest workspace snapshot. Make sure you are still signed into the same account that logged the spend. If the mismatch continues, sign out and back in once to force a clean bootstrap. If you used CSV import or forwarded receipts, check Transactions to see whether the item is still waiting in the review queue instead of already approved.

articles.question: How do CSV imports work in EVA?
articles.summary: CSV rows go into a review queue first so they do not silently overwrite your ledger.
articles.answer: Open Transactions and use Upload CSV. EVA expects a file with date, merchant, and amount columns at minimum. Each valid row becomes a draft transaction in the review queue, where you can approve it, edit it, or reject it before it becomes a real spending event. Duplicate rows are skipped automatically when EVA recognizes the same merchant, date, amount, and source combination.

articles.question: How do I forward receipts into EVA?
articles.summary: Forward receipts to your personal EVA inbox alias so they become reviewable draft transactions.
articles.answer: In Transactions, EVA shows your forwarding alias in the format receipts+your-user-id@useaima.com. Forward email receipts there. EVA ingests the receipt, extracts the merchant, amount, and date when possible, and places the result into your review queue. This keeps the system safe because forwarded receipts never become canonical spending until you approve them yourself.

articles.question: How do budget limits work?
articles.summary: Monthly category limits give EVA a grounded way to warn you before you overspend.
articles.answer: Set monthly limits by category from Budget Limits. EVA compares approved spending in that category against the limit and marks categories as healthy, watch, or over. This makes alerts and advice more useful because EVA can warn you before you drift too far instead of reacting only after the month is gone.

articles.question: How do I update goals after onboarding?
articles.summary: Goals remain editable after setup and feed EVA's progress nudges.
articles.answer: Open Goals and update the target amount, current amount, deadline, or icon any time. EVA recalculates progress percentage, the monthly contribution needed, and whether the goal is still on track. That same status appears in the Dashboard and the recommendation cards EVA uses to guide your next step.

articles.question: What does Generate Financial Statement include?
articles.summary: The statement combines your onboarding baseline, entries, subscriptions, and approved spending data.
articles.answer: Generate Financial Statement uses the data already stored in EVA. It combines your onboarding profile, approved financial entries, recurring subscriptions, and tracked spending to produce a cashflow-style statement with assets, liabilities, income, expenses, and net worth context. If you have very little data, EVA still generates a starter statement, but it may encourage you to add more financial entries so the balance sheet becomes more complete.

articles.question: Why did financial statement generation fail?
articles.summary: Most statement errors come from session problems, low data, or a temporary network issue.
articles.answer: If EVA cannot generate your financial statement, first refresh the page once so the authenticated session and workspace bootstrap reload cleanly. If you just verified your email, wait a moment and try again so the session token settles before the request reaches the backend. If the error keeps repeating, make sure you are still signed into the same account that completed onboarding, and check whether EVA is asking you to enable MFA for this sensitive action first. If the statement still fails after those checks, use the support link from the statement screen and include the exact error text.

articles.question: How are insights generated?
articles.summary: Insights are grounded in your stored records, not invented from thin air.
articles.answer: EVA builds insights from your canonical finance data: approved spending events, budgets, goals, subscriptions, and profile baseline. Daily and weekly summaries are lightweight grounded recaps, while deeper AI insights use the same stored data to highlight category pressure, savings opportunities, and next best actions. When data is still light, EVA should say so clearly and ask you to log more real activity instead of pretending it knows more than it does.

articles.question: How do I update my profile or preferences?
articles.summary: Use the profile menu to open profile, settings, and account controls.
articles.answer: Open the avatar menu in the top-right corner and choose Profile, Account, or Settings depending on what you want to change. Profile is for your personal details and planning defaults. Settings covers appearance, font size, reduced motion, and other app preferences. Account shows your signed-in identity and verification state.

articles.question: Why is EVA asking me to enable MFA for some actions?
articles.summary: EVA only asks for MFA before sensitive actions such as statement generation or approving imported finance data.
articles.answer: EVA does not require MFA for casual browsing. Instead, it steps security up only for sensitive actions like generating a financial statement, approving imported draft transactions, or using receipt-forwarding workflows. To enable MFA, open Settings, go to Account, start the authenticator setup, scan the QR code, and verify the six-digit code from your authenticator app. If you already enabled MFA but EVA still prompts you, use Refresh MFA status once and then try the action again.

articles.question: How do I sign out securely?
articles.summary: Use the profile menu and sign out when leaving shared devices.
articles.answer: Open the profile avatar menu and choose Sign out. EVA clears your current session so someone else cannot reopen your workspace on the same device. If you are on a shared or public machine, always sign out before you leave the browser.

articles.question: What happens if I lose internet while using EVA?
articles.summary: EVA shows a network dialog and asks you to reconnect before retrying.
articles.answer: When EVA cannot reach the server, it shows a network connection dialog instead of silently failing. Reconnect your network first, then choose Try again. If you were in the middle of onboarding, your draft answers stay auto-saved. If you were importing or reviewing transactions, refresh once the connection returns so the latest workspace state reloads.

articles.question: The app feels slow on mobile. What can I do?
articles.summary: Use a stable connection, close heavy tabs, and install the EVA PWA for faster repeat launches.
articles.answer: On slower devices or networks, start by closing heavy background tabs and making sure your connection is stable. If you use EVA often on mobile, install the PWA so repeat launches feel faster and more app-like. If performance stays poor, use the support email and include the device type, browser, and what part of EVA feels slow so the team can reproduce it.
